mechanism_narrative: The month-over-month change in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) captures the marginal health of the labor market; positive changes indicate income growth that drives aggregate demand and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC of PAYEMS changes vs 20-day SPX returns remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or the mean forward return conditional on positive PAYEMS changes is not significantly greater than zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 0.25%, the zero lower bound constraint forces the Fed to rely on unconventional policy; sustained low FEDFUNDS compresses the risk-free rate and pushes investors out the risk curve, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are not significantly positive following months when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25% (t-test p > 0.05), or the strategy Sharpe ratio underperforms the buy-and-hold benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising industrial production (INDPRO) and declining unemployment (UNRATE) simultaneously confirms a supply-demand recovery; the joint event outperforms either signal alone in predicting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of INDPRO and UNRATE produces a Rank IC below 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window, or the interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) in a bivariate regression against 20-day forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) growth generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when CPI inflation is low and the federal funds rate is accommodative, because demand strength lifts earnings without threatening tighter monetary policy.
regime_hint: low inflation with accommodative monetary policy
failure_condition: In months with below-median CPIAUCSL and accommodative FEDFUNDS, RSAFS growth fails to predict positive S&P 500 20-day returns (Rank IC < 0.03), or the conditional Sharpe ratio is not statistically higher than the unconditional Sharpe (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points over a trailing 3-month window signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending and corporate earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are not significantly negative following a 0.5pp UNRATE spike (mean return >= 0 or t-test p > 0.10), or the signal fails to trigger in at least 5% of historical months.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly growth in industrial production (INDPRO) directly proxies for corporate output and operating leverage; sustained increases in INDPRO translate into positive earnings surprises and drive S&P 500 returns higher over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC of INDPRO month-over-month changes vs 5-day SPX returns is below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive INDPRO changes are not followed by positive mean 5-day returns (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 1% and housing starts (HOUST) are rising month-over-month, the combined monetary stimulus and construction recovery generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition triggers in fewer than 10% of months, or forward 60-day returns following the joint trigger are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Acceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) growth relative to the prior quarter signals strengthening consumer demand that supports top-line revenue growth for S&P 500 constituents, producing positive forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC of RSAFS changes vs 20-day SPX returns is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive RSAFS surprises fail to coincide with positive 20-day forward returns (hit rate < 55%).
